data.title: REG - Kingfisher PLC - Director/PDMR Shareholding
data.description:
RNS Number : 8073E Kingfisher PLC 24 October 2025  

KINGFISHER PLC

Notification of Transactions of Directors/Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibility ('PDMRs')

Grant of awards under the Kingfisher Performance Share Plan (the 'KPSP') and the KPSP French Free Share Sub Plan (the 'PSP French Sub Plan')

24 October 2025: Kingfisher plc (the 'Company') announces that, on 23 October 2025, awards were granted to PDMRs to acquire ordinary shares of 15 5/7 pence each in the Company (the 'Shares') under the KPSP and the PSP French Sub Plan.

The price per share used to calculate the number of Shares over which the awards have been granted is 303.10 pence (being the average of the closing price of the Shares on the main market of the London Stock Exchange over the three trading days prior to the date of grant (20, 21 and 22 October 2025)).

These awards were made in accordance with the Directors' Remuneration Policy and KPSP Rules approved by shareholders at the Company's 2022 AGM and where appropriate, the PSP French Sub Plan Rules.The awards are subject, where applicable, to continued employment and the achievement of performance conditions. Additional detail regarding the awards granted to Bhavesh Mistry will be disclosed within the Directors' Remuneration Report of the Company's 2025/26 Annual Report.

Following these grants, Bhavesh holds the following interests in the Company's issued share capital:

data.title: GSK Wins FDA Approval for Blood-Cancer Drug Blenrep — Update
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

GSK won approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for blood-cancer drug Blenrep, paving the way for a return to the market of a medicine the company sees as a potential blockbuster.

The FDA nod comes after an advisory committee voted against approval of Blenrep in July, which cast doubt on whether GSK would be able to reintroduce the drug in the U.S. Drug regulators gave accelerated approval to Blenrep in 2020, but later revoked the decision when a clinical trial showed the drug--then used alone--didn't outperform an existing treatment.

The FDA approved Blenrep as part of a drug combination for adult patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have received at least two prior lines of therapy. It also said the drug would be available only through a risk evaluation and mitigation strategy, a program that restricts how medicines can be marketed to enhance safety.

Shares in GSK fell as much as 3% before recovering somewhat, trading down 1.2% in European afternoon Friday.

Blenrep's approval had been in doubt after the advisory committee vote and the FDA's decision to allow the drug to return to the U.S. market is positive for GSK, but a highly limited label likely reduces the commercial opportunity, analysts at UBS wrote in a note to clients.

GSK, which has previously estimated the drug's annual peak sales could top 3 billion pounds ($4 billion), said it continues to work on a clinical program to demonstrate Blenrep's potential benefits in earlier lines of treatment.

The company's chief scientific officer, Tony Wood, said Blenrep fulfills a major patient need and that it can redefine treatment for multiple myeloma patients.

The approval was based on results of a late-stage clinical trial that showed a reduction in the risk of death and tripled median progression-free survival, the company said.

GSK said the U.S. approval for Blenrep follows prior authorizations in the European Union, the U.K., Japan, Canada, Switzerland and Brazil.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Nomination Committee of Avanza Bank Holding AB (publ)
data.description:
The following principles for the Nomination Committee apply as resolved by the Annual General Meeting of Avanza Bank Holding AB (publ). The Nomination Committee’s members shall include one representative for each of the four largest shareholders, together with Avanza Bank Holding AB’s Chairman of the Board. The names of the members of the Nomination Committee and the owners they represent shall be published no later than six months before the Annual General Meeting and shall be based on the known ownership structure immediately prior to publication.

The four shareholders controlling the largest number of votes and who wish to appoint a representative ahead of the Annual General Meeting 2026 are, according to Avanza Bank Holding AB’s knowledge: Biovestor AB (company controlled by Sven Hagströmer with family), Creades AB, AMF - Tjänstepensioner och Fonder, and the Dybeck family with companies.

The represented shareholders have decided that the Nomination Committee shall consist of the Chairman of the Board Sven Hagströmer, representing Creades AB, Erik Törnberg representing Biovestor AB, Dick Bergqvist representing AMF - Tjänstepensioner och Fonder, and Magnus Dybeck representing the Dybeck family with companies. Erik Törnberg has been appointed Chairman of the Nomination Committee.

The Annual General Meeting will be held in Stockholm on 28 April 2026.

Shareholders who would like to submit a proposal to the Nomination Committee should do so at the latest by 27 January 2026 via e-mail to valberedningen@avanza.se, or by post to:

Avanza Bank Holding AB

Att: Valberedningen

Box 1399

111 93 Stockholm, Sweden

For further information please contact:

Erik Törnberg, Chairman of the Nomination Committee Tel: +46 (0) 8-412 011 00

Avanza is a digital platform for savings and investments, founded in 1999. The Parent Company, Avanza Bank Holding AB (publ), is listed on the Stockholm Stock Exchange. Avanza’s customer promise is a better return on your savings than with any other bank or pension provider in Sweden. Services include saving in shares, funds, savings accounts, mortgages, and a strong pension offering. Avanza has over 2.2 million customers with over SEK 1,000 billion in total savings capital. This is equivalent to 7.9 per cent of the Swedish savings market. Avanza is largest in terms of number of transactions among Swedish banks on the Stockholm Stock Exchange. During the last 15 years Avanza has won SKI’s (Swedish Quality Index) award, “Year’s Most Satisfied Savings Customers”. For more information visit: avanza.se/ir

data.title: Getting Started With API RP 1185: Prioritizing Public Engagement for Pipeline Success
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / October 24, 2025 / The landscape of the energy industry is rapidly evolving and so are the expectations placed on pipeline operators. Communities, regulators, and investors alike are demanding more than technical excellence; they want transparency, inclusion, and a visible commitment to developing public trust. In this context, effective public engagement has become a strategic imperative rather than a communications afterthought.

To help companies meet these expectations, the American Petroleum Institute (API) has introduced API Recommended Practice (RP) 1185: Pipeline Public Engagement. This recommended practice offers practical guidance for how pipeline operators can foster meaningful relationships with local officials, landowners, tribal governments, environmental organizations, and other stakeholders. It complements and enhances the safety-focused API RP 1173 by addressing the human side of infrastructure development.

For organizations looking to implement RP 1185, the biggest question is often: Where do we begin and how do we prioritize our efforts? Below, we break down the fundamentals of RP 1185, offer a clear starting point for implementation, and share how Antea Group can be your trusted partner in the process.

Demystifying RP 1185: What It Really Means

At its core, API RP 1185 is about integrating public engagement into the fabric of your pipeline operations. It encourages proactive, transparent, and inclusive communication with stakeholders before, during, and after project execution. The recommended practice is flexible, scalable, and designed to meet the unique needs of companies operating in diverse regulatory environments and community contexts.

The emphasis is on relationship-building. That means not just providing information, but listening, responding, and building mutual understanding. Successful implementation of RP 1185 is recognized to enhance safety outcomes, mitigate reputational risk, and reinforce the social license to operate.

How to Get Started: A Strategic Path Forward

Similar to API RP 1173, launching a successful RP 1185 program begins with internal alignment. Senior leadership must treat public engagement as a strategic priority, not just a compliance task. Establishing a cross-functional implementation team typically composed of representatives from EHS, Public Affairs, Legal, and Operations can help ensure consistent messaging and unified action across the organization.

From there, the next critical step is identifying your stakeholders. While it's easy to focus on regulatory agencies and landowners, an effective approach will also consider local emergency responders, community advocates, environmental NGOs, internal employees, tribal representatives, and even local media. Understanding who your stakeholders are, what they care about, and how they prefer to communicate allows you to tailor your engagement efforts for greater effectiveness.

Once stakeholders are identified, it's important to conduct a baseline assessment. What public engagement activities is your organization already participating in? Where are the strengths? Where are the gaps when compared to the principles of RP 1185? This is where Antea Group can add significant value with our EHS consultants performing a detailed gap analysis and coming alongside our clients to generate a roadmap for improvement.

The insights gained during this assessment phase feed directly into the development of a tailored engagement strategy. This strategy should be rooted in clear, measurable goals. Are you seeking to increase community understanding of pipeline safety? Reduce local opposition to a new project? Establish long-term partnerships with Indigenous communities? Whatever the aim, your strategy should also include key messaging themes, selected communication channels, and well-defined feedback mechanisms to ensure dialogue is a two-way street.

Prioritization is essential, especially when resources are limited. Start by focusing on areas with the highest potential impact, communities near new construction, densely populated regions, environmentally sensitive zones, or locations with a history of public concern. While it's important to capture early wins through high-visibility efforts, it's equally vital to plan for long-term, sustained engagement.

Implementation requires more than a written plan. Staff at all levels, especially those with community-facing roles, should be trained on the critical RP 1185 principles, effective communication techniques, and cultural competency. This will ensure that your team speaks with one voice, builds trust through consistency, and knows how to respond when tensions or concerns arise.

Finally, like any good management system, public engagement should be tracked and continuously improved. Organizations must establish ways to monitor progress, capture stakeholder feedback, and adjust their strategies based on what they learn. Metrics might include public sentiment, the number of resolved community concerns, attendance at engagement events, or the frequency of two-way communications. This iterative approach mirrors the continuous improvement cycle emphasized within RP 1173, reinforcing the alignment between safety and public trust.

A well-executed public engagement strategy offers substantial benefits including strengthening relationships within communities, reducing project delays and most importantly building long-term trust that translates into safer operations and stronger reputations.

Public engagement done right is a risk management tool, a reputation builder, and catalyst for operational success. It allows operators to proactively address issues before they escalate, transforming potential conflict into collaboration.

We offer services that span the full implementation lifecycle from stakeholder identification and engagement planning to training, reporting, and performance monitoring. Whether you're launching your first engagement initiative or looking to enhance an existing program, we help you set meaningful goals, communicate effectively, and demonstrate impact to both communities and investors.

Final Thoughts

API RP 1185 is not just another guideline, it's a forward-looking framework for building trust, fostering collaboration, and driving safer, more successful pipeline operations. In an era of increasing scrutiny and stakeholder activism, companies that engage thoughtfully and transparently will be better positioned to succeed.

data.title: Canadian Domestic Rail Volumes Offset Weakness in U.S. — Market Talk
data.description:
0953 ET - While the volume of rail freight Canada receives from its nearest neighbor is down, domestic carloadings are helping counter that. Statistics Canada data shows the total volume of rail freight carried in Canada hit 30.2 million metric tons in August, up 4.5% in year and slightly above the five-year historical average of 30.1 million tons in August. The rise was driven by higher volumes of domestic loadings, both non-intermodal and intermodal. Freight coming in from the U.S. was down 7.1% at 3.4 million tons, as rail imports sank 13.9% and exports dropped 8.1% to the U.S. Of note, the data also shows the volume of canola moved by rail at 297,000 tons was the lowest in three years as the value of exports of the commodity sank 46.5% on-year after China imposed steep tariffs on Canadian canola. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0941 ET - Inflation metrics are likely to improve in the near future, LPL Financial's Jeffrey Roach writes following September's mild CPI report. He says tariffs were likely the culprit for a steep 0.7% rise in apparel prices in September. Roach cites the Fed's Beige Book saying that some firms are eating up tariff-induced costs to preserve market share. Meanwhile, housing prices "are decelerating and should slow further amid the growing supply of multifamily housing and the shrinking demand from lower immigration," he says. Roach expects the Fed to continue easing throughout 2026. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0937 ET - Before Rogers Communications can unlock the value from Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, it first needs to buy the remaining 25% owned by Kilmer Sports. Scotiabank's Maher Yaghi says in a report that the current estimate for the quarter-interest in MLSE is C$3.3 billion. Rogers would then need to sell a minority interest to private equity or launch an IPO to unlock upside, he says. Yaghi thinks this could happen in the second half of next year, and "if done successfully this could unlock around C$5 a share in the stock." (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0928 ET - September's inflation report "is a sigh of relief for the Fed and won't derail what is likely a second consecutive rate cut" next week, although inflation remains above the 2% target, Fitch's Olu Sonola writes. "As odd as it may seem, the Fed will be happy with inflation staying around 3% for the next couple of months." Sonola says the tariff passthrough "generally remains muted." Meanwhile, labor data are still missing, leaving markets to hope that the government shutdown will be over by the December monetary policy meeting and "the Fed has a clearer read on jobs," Sonola says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0927 ET - GSK won approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its blood-cancer drug Blenrep, securing a positive outcome in a decision that had been in doubt that is somewhat overshadowed by the medicine's limited label, UBS analysts say. The FDA approved Blenrep as a third line of treatments--for adult patients who had received at least two prior therapies--, instead of the second line GSK was hoping for, UBS says. Moreover, patients require an ophthalmic exam before every dose, which will come as a disappointment for GSK, according to UBS. "Whilst it is a positive for GSK that Blenrep will return to the U.S. market, the smaller indication and stringent [risk evaluation and mitigation strategy] likely reduces the commercial opportunity," the analysts say. Shares fall 1.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0922 ET - Canada's competitiveness as an auto manufacturer is rapidly eroding, warns the Canadian Vehicle Manufacturers' Association. President Brian Kingston in a statement says his association supports Ottawa's goal of protecting the auto industry and its workers, but adds tariffs on Canadian vehicle manufacturers when they are already under pressure only makes a difficult situation worse. It comes after Canada moved to cut the number of vehicles Stellantis and General Motors can import tariff-free in response to company plans to reduce their manufacturing footprint in Canada. Kingston says that combined with legal threats and EV mandates, Canada should instead focus on strengthening the auto industry. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

0914 ET - O'Reilly Automotive alluded to some upcoming weakness in the autoparts sector, but DA Davidson's Michael Baker says it's still a good business under the hood. The analyst notes that the stock dipped during O'Reilly's quarterly call due to comments on slowing transaction trends in their DIY business in some bigger ticket maintenance areas and on comments that the inflation-related benefits to comparatives may be peaking. Despite isolated softness in some DIY big-ticket categories, Baker says the overall business remains strong, with DIFM comparatives up over 10% and DIY holding steady in the low single digits. Baker says that O'Reilly's 4Q guidance implies a notable two-year acceleration, while historical patterns suggest post-earnings dips have consistently led to outperformance. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0905 ET - A benign September inflation report keeps the Fed on pace to cut interest rates "at least for the next two meetings" of this year, Regan Capital's Skyler Weinand writes. Higher inflation would have made it harder for the central bank to ease monetary policy to support jobs. Weinand expects the Fed to take a pause "to review a few months of data before cutting again in 2026." He adds that the delayed September CPI, which was slightly cooler than expected, is "real and based on real data from September, however, the collection and reliability of the data continues to weaken." He expects alternative data sources to gain relevance going forward. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0851 ET - Treasury yields decline and stock futures rise as the much-awaited U.S. September inflation report shows the 12-month pace accelerating to 3% from 2.9% as of August, and core slowing to 3% from 3.1%. Economists surveyed by WSJ expected 3.1% for both indicators. Futures markets massively price in a second 25-basis-point cut by the Fed Wednesday. As the government shutdown shows no signs of ending, the CPI report could be the only official indicator Wall Street will see for a while. The 10-year yield is at 3.976% and the two-year at 3.453%, both slightly lower than yesterday. Futures for the S$P 500 and Nasdaq both rise around 1%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0835 ET - The Food and Drug Administration's approval of GSK's Blenrep blood-cancer treatment marks a major milestone for the British pharma company, though it is unlikely to deliver an immediate boost to earnings, AJ Bell analyst Russ Mould writes. Blenrep could significantly grow sales in the U.S., where GSK is investing heavily in research and development as well as supply-chain infrastructure, Mould says. This should come as good news after a recent negative opinion on the drug from a U.S. advisory committee raised investor doubts over whether it would be approved, Mould writes. But the treatment's benefit to profit will take time to assess, Mould adds. Shares fall 1.5%. (william.gray@wsj.com)

0824 ET - Rogers Communications' new mobile subscribers are being onboarded at higher price points, suggesting the price war in the telecom sphere may be normalizing. In a TD Cowen report, Vince Valentini says pricing seems to have reached an inflection point and new gross adds for Rogers look to be coming in at higher prices than where they would have been six to nine months ago. "While this front book pricing may not yet exceed the average of back book pricing, we see a clear road map towards the stabilization in average revenue per unit in 2H/26 should wireless pricing continue to be rational," Valentini says. Still, he cautions that this isn't set in stone, with Black Friday promotions around the corner. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0818 ET - West Fraser Timber is nimble enough to navigate whatever waters might come in the next few months. RBC's Matthew McKellar says in a report that West Fraser's low-cost focus, advantaged softwood lumber duty rate, geographical diversification, and strong balance sheet position it to withstand a variety of scenarios that could play out in wood products markets into 2026. Additionally, the analyst things that the company could also be a potential acquirer of high-quality assets that could come to market should a period of more challenging conditions continue.(adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

data.title: UK seizes record haul of illegal weight-loss drugs in factory raid
data.description:
By Sam Tabahriti

Britain's medicines regulator said on Friday it had made the world's largest seizure of unlicensed weight-loss medicines, dismantling a factory that made jabs labelled as containing the ingredient in Eli Lilly's Mounjaro.

Global pharmaceutical companies and regulators have raised alarms over a surge in counterfeit and unregulated weight-loss drugs, which are being trafficked globally and sold illegally online and through underground networks.

The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) said it had seized 2,000 injection pens labelled as containing tirzepatide - the ingredient in Eli Lilly's Mounjaro and Zepbound - and retatrutide, an experimental obesity drug also made by Eli Lilly, which is still in trials.

Tens of thousands of empty pens were also seized, along with raw chemicals.

Eli Lilly did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

FIRST FACTORY DISCOVERY IN UK

The agency said the factory in central England was the first of its kind discovered in Britain.

"This is a victory in the fight against the shameless criminals who are putting lives at risk by peddling dangerous and illegal weight loss jabs to make a quick buck," health minister Wes Streeting said in a statement.

"These unregulated products, made with no regard for safety or quality, posed a major risk to unwitting customers."

Both tirzepatide and retatrutide mimic hormones that suppress appetite and regulate blood sugar.

Britain's National Health Service prescribes weight-loss medicines only to patients who meet certain criteria. For those who obtain them privately, the drugs can cost hundreds of pounds per month.

In the United States, some people have begun sourcing raw ingredients online to self-manufacture GLP-1-based injections such as semaglutide - a drug developed by Novo Nordisk and used in Ozempic and Wegovy - citing high retail prices and limited access.

The trend has raised safety concerns among health officials, who warn that DIY formulations pose serious health risks due to contamination, incorrect dosing and lack of oversight.

($1 = 0.7451 pounds)

data.title: Colombian man sentenced to 42 years for murder and dismemberment of UK couple
data.description:
A Colombian national who murdered two men in west London and transported parts of their dismembered bodies in suitcases to southwest England was sentenced on Friday to 42 years in prison.

Yostin Andres Mosquera, 35, was convicted in July of killing Albert Alfonso, 62, and Paul Longworth, 71, at their flat in July 2024. Prosecutors said Mosquera had bludgeoned Longworth with a hammer and stabbed Alfonso to death after the pair had sex.

He then decapitated and dismembered both victims, placing parts of their bodies into suitcases and travelling to Bristol, southwest England, where he abandoned the luggage on the Clifton Suspension Bridge.

He had pleaded not guilty to two counts of murder but admitted to the manslaughter of Alfonso, claiming he had lost control after Alfonso killed Longworth and threatened his family. The jury rejected his defence and found him guilty of both murders.

On Friday, Mosquera pleaded guilty to three new charges of possessing indecent images of children.

Judge Joel Bennathan, who described the murders as "thoroughly wicked crimes," handed down his sentence of life imprisonment with a minimum term of 42 years at Woolwich Crown Court on Friday.

data.title: U.K. to Reopen Sept 2038 Index-Linked Gilt Via Syndication in Week Starting Nov. 10
data.description:
By Miriam Mukuru

The U.K. Debt Management Office plans to reopen the September 2038 index-linked gilt through syndication in the week starting Nov. 10, subject to demand and market conditions, it said on Friday.

This will be the fifth syndication by the DMO in fiscal 2026. The DMO has raised a total of 32.4 billion pounds ($43.2 billion) through the previous four syndications in fiscal 2026, it said.

"It is expected that the upcoming syndication will be a modestly-sized transaction," the DMO said.

Write to Miriam Mukuru at miriam.mukuru@wsj.com

data.title: P&G Stock Rises on Earnings. Why Tariffs Are Still a Factor to Watch. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Shares of Procter & Gamble rose Friday after the maker of consumer products reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings.

The company posted core earnings of $1.99 a share for its fiscal first quarter, surpassing analysts' consensus estimate of $1.90. Sales totaled $22.4 billion, above Wall Street's call for $20.9 billion and up 3% from the prior year.

P&G stock was up 2.4% in premarket trading Friday. Shares have fallen 9.8% this year as of Thursday's close.

"These results keep us on track to deliver within our guidance ranges on all key financial metrics for the fiscal year in a challenging consumer and geopolitical environment," said CEO Jon Moeller in a statement.

P&G now expects to incur higher costs from tariffs of around $400 million after tax. However, the company reiterated its fiscal 2026 outlook for sales growth of between 1% and 5% and for core per-share earnings of $6.83 to $7.09.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
